summary: Stellar Scarlet>Fire sequence & thunderous Stella Blue finale
blurb: The second set roars with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition and a soaring Stella Blue closer. Brent Mydland’s piano shines on Foolish Heart while Bill Kreutzmann delivers tight drumming throughout. Highlights also include Hell In A Bucket, Candyman and Dark Star.
review: June 28‑1988 at Saratoga Performing Arts Center captured one of the Dead’s late‑’80s high points. The first set opened hot with “Hell In A Bucket” and rolled through a fiery “Candyman,” while Brent Mydland added melodic depth on “Foolish Heart.” The second set truly ignited: a blistering “Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain” segue built into an expansive “Estimated Prophet,” and the band kept the momentum through “I Will Take You Home,” “Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad,” and “I Need A Miracle.” The night culminated in a thunder‑thick “Stella Blue” that sent the crowd homeward, followed by a heartfelt encore of “Knockin' On Heaven's Door.” Across multiple recordings the performance is praised for its energy, tight transitions, and standout solos—particularly Jerry’s soaring leads on “Candyman,” Brent’s piano work on “Foolish Heart,” and Bill’s crisp drumming. Even the audience‑recorded “Dark Star” captures the improvisational spark that made this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six recordings describe the show positively (epic Scarlet>Fire, stellar Stella Blue finale, strong second set) while two are mixed; no consistent negative commentary appears. The consensus of high‑energy playing and multiple standout moments justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with tight drumming and balanced mix, preserving the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Blazing Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition
Thunderous Stella Blue finale that lifts the whole show
Brent Mydland’s piano shines on Foolish Heart and Candyman
Bill Kreutzmann delivers tight, driving drumming throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers rolling sequences and soaring solos—especially a tasty lead on Row Jimmy, blistering work during Candyman, and commanding presence throughout the Scarlet>Fire jam.
band_performance.Brent: Piano shines with extended melodic sections in Foolish Heart, tight interplay on Stella Blue, and energetic cuts loose on Candyman and other numbers despite some mix limitations.
band_performance.Billy: Praised for tight, well‑executed drumming that anchors the high‑energy set and adds to the epic texture of the second half.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
